accept: to receive or agree to something willingly
ancient: very old; from the distant past
arcane: understood by few; mysterious or secret
arrogant: having an exaggerated sense of one's own importance
astute: shrewd; quick to understand and act
augment: to make larger by adding to it
barren: unable to produce growth or life; empty
brave: ready to face danger or pain; courageous
careful: giving close attention to avoid mistakes or harm
cat: a small domesticated feline animal, often kept as a pet
cautious: careful to avoid danger or mistakes
chisel: a bladed hand tool struck to cut or shape wood or stone
clear: easy to see, understand, or perceive
concise: giving much information in few words; brief but clear
constitution: the fundamental laws by which a state is governed; also bodily strength
court: the place where judges hear legal cases; also to seek someone's affection
criticize: to point out faults; to find fault with
cryptic: having a hidden or obscure meaning
cut: to divide or open something with a sharp edge
defeat: to win victory over; to beat
delicate: fine and easily damaged; requiring careful handling
doubt: a feeling of uncertainty about whether something is true
eager: keenly wanting to do something; enthusiastic
early: before the usual or expected time
easy: achieved without great effort; not difficult
enormous: very large in size or quantity
ephemeral: lasting a very short time; fleeting
esoteric: understood by only a small, specialized group
fast: moving or able to move quickly
fertile: able to produce abundant growth or life
fleeting: lasting only a very short time
flexible: able to bend or adapt easily
food: anything eaten to provide the body with energy and nutrients
frank: open, honest, and direct in speech
friendly: kind and pleasant; warm in manner
frugal: sparing or economical with money or resources
genuine: truly what it is said to be; authentic
happy: feeling or showing pleasure or contentment
hiss: the sharp s-like sound a snake or escaping air makes
hive: a structured home where bees live and store honey
hour: a unit of time equal to sixty minutes
increase: to become greater in size or number
ineluctable: unable to be avoided or escaped; inevitable
insects: small six-legged animals such as ants, bees and beetles
lavish: very generous or extravagant, often to excess
light: bright; full of light, or of little weight
magnanimous: generous and forgiving, especially toward a rival
malicious: intending to do harm out of spite
mitigate: to make less severe or painful
modern: of the present time; recent in style or method
murky: dark and gloomy; hard to see through
mysterious: difficult to understand or explain; puzzling
nascent: just coming into existence; beginning to develop
nest: a structure a bird builds to hold its eggs and young
obdurate: stubbornly refusing to change one's mind
obscure: not clearly understood or expressed; hidden
old: having lived or existed for a long time
opaque: not able to be seen through; not transparent
pack: a group of animals, especially wolves, that live and hunt together
paintings: pictures made by applying paint to a surface
peaceful: calm and free from disturbance or conflict
perceptive: quick to notice and understand things
petty: of little importance; trivial or small-minded
praise: to express warm approval or admiration
pressure: the force pressing on a surface; also stress or urgency
pride: deep satisfaction in achievements; also a group of lions
prolific: producing much fruit, work, or offspring
prudent: acting with care and good judgment
quick: moving fast or done in a short time
rigid: stiff and unable to bend; inflexible
scarce: in short supply; hard to find
scatter: to throw or spread in different directions
school: a place of teaching; also a large group of fish swimming together
score: the points made in a game; also a group of twenty
second: a unit of time, one sixtieth of a minute; also the position after first
shoe: a shaped outer covering worn on the foot
shrink: to become smaller in size or amount
sleep: the natural resting state in which awareness of the world pauses
slow: moving or operating at low speed
sociable: enjoying the company of others; friendly
sock: a soft covering worn on the foot inside a shoe
stable: firmly fixed; not likely to change
stingy: unwilling to spend or give; mean
student: a person who is studying, especially at a school or university
swift: moving or able to move very fast
symphony: a long musical work for full orchestra, usually in several movements
temperature: the measure of how hot or cold something is
terse: brief and to the point, sometimes curtly so
transient: lasting only a short time; fleeting
transparent: able to be seen through; clear
tree: a tall plant with a wooden trunk and branches
unyielding: not giving way; firm and inflexible
victory: success in a contest or struggle
volatile: liable to change rapidly and unpredictably
weapons: instruments made or used for fighting or attack
winter: the coldest season of the year
zealous: showing great energy and enthusiasm for a cause
